headline: 467 NASAO airframes grounded across 25 states with $50M–$2B replacement exposure while gray zone entities face parallel regulatory collapse

lead_story.title: NASAO Grounding Crisis Exposes $2B Replacement Gap
lead_story.body: Oregon's NASAO Revision 2 white paper (February 2026) documents 467 drone airframes grounded or operationally restricted across 23 states based on a survey of 25 state DOTs, creating national replacement exposure between $50M and $2B. This represents the largest documented fleet disruption in state UAS operations to date. The grounding coincides with FCC Covered List restrictions eliminating major gray zone suppliers (Anzu, SkyRover, Autel) from procurement pipelines, compressing available replacement options into an already strained Blue UAS supply base. The timing creates a perfect storm: massive replacement demand hitting a supply chain with 64% concentration risk in companion computers and 59% in mesh radios.
lead_story.action: Conduct immediate fleet audit to quantify your agency's exposure to NASAO-affected airframes and gray zone entities. Submit prioritized replacement requirements to your Blue UAS Framework vendors by April 25 to secure allocation before Q3 bottlenecks.

gray_zone.status: FCC Covered List entity with critical supply chain compromise (risk score 0.850)
gray_zone.development: Teardown analysis confirms SkyRover app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure with identical encryption keys. OTA update pipeline routes through adversary infrastructure, meaning firmware updates and potentially operational data flow through servers under foreign control. Shell company filings trace to SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd. S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) models remain available at retail despite FCC prohibition on new authorizations.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Any agency operating SkyRover S1/X1 has live data links to DJI infrastructure with no jurisdictional oversight. State and local agencies that purchased pre-ban inventory retain operational but non-compliant assets.
gray_zone.action: Ground all SkyRover units immediately. Do not perform firmware updates. Treat all flight data and imagery from these platforms as potentially compromised. Initiate emergency procurement action for compliant replacements under expedited authority if operationally critical.

gray_zone.status: Texas DTPA lawsuit filed Feb 18, 2026; no response filed after 45+ days (risk score 0.782)
gray_zone.development: Collin County Texas lawsuit documents 7 counts under Deceptive Trade Practices Act. Teardown evidence shows PCB layouts, board part numbers, and controller (RRC01) match DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise exactly. Firmware remains signed with DJI cryptographic keys, meaning DJI retains root control over all Anzu airframes. Product line discontinued due to adversary component shortage, leaving existing buyers with orphaned platforms. State seeks injunctive relief and up to $10K per violation in civil penalties.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Estimated 2,000+ Raptor series airframes deployed across state/local agencies and critical infrastructure operators. All units retain DJI firmware signature and lack independent update capability. No parts pipeline for repairs.
gray_zone.action: Freeze all Anzu Raptor procurement and operations pending lawsuit resolution. Document all Anzu assets for potential replacement budget justification. Monitor Texas AG action for precedent that may trigger federal procurement restrictions. Plan 90-day phase-out.

gray_zone.status: FCC Covered List entity as of Dec 22, 2025; no new authorizations permitted (risk score 0.537)
gray_zone.development: Teardown confirms Specta Air uses identical PCBs to DJI Air 3 with same DJI P1 chipset. RF emission reports differ only by date and logo, indicating minimal to no independent engineering. Technology licensing from DJI undisclosed in marketing materials. FCC retroactive revocation authority (granted Oct 2025) applies to all existing authorizations.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Small but growing deployment in academic research programs and commercial inspection contracts. Primary risk is regulatory non-compliance rather than fleet scale. Secondary risk is supply chain dead-end with no upgrade path.
gray_zone.action: Treat Specta inventory as non-compliant for federal contracts or federally-funded programs. State/local programs should verify FCC authorization status before any new deployments. Existing users should budget for replacement within 12 months.

gray_zone.status: Named in FY2025 NDAA Section 1709(a)(1); FCC revoked certain authorizations Jan 2026 (risk score 0.525)
gray_zone.development: Unlike DJI derivatives, Autel uses proprietary architecture but faces identical FCC Covered List restrictions. Certain pre-ban FCC authorizations revoked in January 2026 administrative action. Company seeking reconsideration at FCC level. Dragonfish fixed-wing platform has notable deployment in sheriff's offices and public safety (Florida documented Aug 2021, Tulsa documented Mar 2026). EVO II series remains in field use.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Moderate deployment in public safety and critical infrastructure. Larger agencies (county sheriff, state DOT) have multi-unit fleets. Risk is regulatory compliance rather than hardware compromise, but replacement pipeline uncertainty creates operational continuity risk.
gray_zone.action: Audit Autel fleet for specific model FCC authorization status. Identify mission-critical uses and develop replacement roadmap targeting 18-month transition. Do not expand Autel footprint. Monitor FCC reconsideration proceedings but do not delay contingency planning.

supply_chain.signal: 82% probability of allocation-only status for defense by Q3 2026. 16 of 25 companion computer platforms in Forge DB use Jetson family (64% concentration). 13 boards currently at risk.
supply_chain.window: 5 months until probable allocation trigger. Lead time for Orin NX already 16-20 weeks as of Q1 2026.
supply_chain.action: Submit 12-month demand forecast to your integrator by April 30. Pre-position 6-month safety stock if budget authority permits. Qualify alternative companion computers (QRB5165, RK3588) for non-critical platforms by end of Q2.

supply_chain.signal: 81% probability of bottleneck in Q3–Q4 2026. 17 of 29 mesh radio options in Forge DB use concentrated supplier base (59%). Only 29 NDAA-compliant options tracked total.
supply_chain.window: 6-9 months before critical shortage. Current lead times 8-12 weeks, rising.
supply_chain.action: Lock in mesh radio supply agreements now for Q4 2026 and Q1 2027 deliveries. Specifically address NDAA compliance certification in contracts. Consider dual-source strategy even if unit cost increases 15-20%. Test interoperability between mesh systems before August.

supply_chain.signal: 87% probability of allocation tightening in H2 2026. 15 of 52 thermal camera platforms use Boson family (29% concentration). AeroVironment Switchblade production ramp identified as primary demand driver.
supply_chain.window: 8-10 months before allocation constraints bind. Boson 640 already preferred spec for Blue UAS thermal payloads.
supply_chain.action: Engage Teledyne FLIR channel partners to establish allocation priority by May 15. Document mission-critical requirements with specific unit projections. For new platform designs, evaluate Boson+ or TAU 2 alternatives to reduce Boson 640 dependency. Do not assume spot market availability after Q3.

watch_list.item: Raspberry Pi diversion to Russian Geran/Molniya drone programs
watch_list.why: GUR confirmed 40,000+ RPi 4B units diverted to Geran-2/3 'Borscht Tracker' and RPi 5 in Molniya-2R reconnaissance drone, creating potential export control escalation
watch_list.trigger: US Commerce Department adds Raspberry Pi products to EAR controls or establishes end-use certification requirements for commercial-off-the-shelf compute modules

watch_list.item: FCC firmware certification deadline convergence in January 2027
watch_list.why: 79% probability that 3 simultaneous regulatory deadlines will ground uncertified gray zone drones in single 30-day window
watch_list.trigger: FCC publishes final compliance timeline with specific manufacturer deadline dates, or first major agency reports operational grounding due to firmware non-compliance

watch_list.why: 5 of 25 companion computer platforms depend on QRB5165; end-of-life announcement would eliminate 20% of Blue UAS processing options with 18-24 month qualification timeline for replacement
watch_list.trigger: Qualcomm product change notification (PCN) for QRB5165 or announcement of RB7 series targeting robotics/UAS market

predictions.event: FLIR Boson 640 moves to allocation-only for defense contracts
predictions.hedge: Execute umbrella purchase agreement with Teledyne FLIR-authorized distributor by end of Q2 2026 establishing minimum quarterly allocation. Qualify secondary thermal core (Sierra-Olympic Viento or similar) for non-targeting applications by August.

predictions.event: NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX enters allocation management for defense customers
predictions.hedge: Pre-position 6-month inventory based on program-of-record projections. Establish direct relationship with NVIDIA defense account team by May. Fund non-recurring engineering to qualify Qualcomm QRB5165 as alternate for two lowest-tier platforms.

predictions.event: FCC firmware compliance deadline grounds uncertified gray zone platforms in concentrated 30-day window
predictions.hedge: Assume zero operational availability of Anzu, SkyRover, Cogito, and non-compliant Autel platforms by Jan 1, 2027. Accelerate all replacement procurement by pulling from FY27 Q2 budgets into FY26 Q4. Identify emergency rental/lease options for mission-critical gaps.

signal_summary.top_concern: 467 grounded NASAO airframes colliding with gray zone supplier collapse
